perturbation.original_text: Each of the parties hereto agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party or its Associates except in connection with preparing a proposal for and any discussions of a Transaction or as otherwise permitted hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the parties hereto agree not to use the Protected Information disclosed to it by the other party or its Associates except in connection with preparing a proposal for and any discussions of a Transaction or as otherwise permitted hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Confidential Information" with "Protected Information" in this section, while the rest of the agreement uses "Confidential Information," creates ambiguity. It's unclear if "Protected Information" is a subset or superset of "Confidential Information," or an entirely different category. This contradicts the definition and usage of "Confidential Information" throughout the rest of the agreement. Specifically the entire agreement is based on defining what is "Confidential Information", and if something is not, then this clause makes the text contradictory
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information; Use and Disclosure . “ Confidential Information ” means any information, technical data or know-how, including, but not limited to, that which relates to research, product or service plans, business practices, agreement terms, products, services, employees, suppliers, customers, technology or other strategic partners, stockholders, markets, software, know-how, developments, inventions, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, notes, analyses or studies and all tangible and intangible embodiments thereof of any kind whatsoever, whether conveyed in writing or orally by the disclosing party or its Associates to the receiving party or its Associates in connection with the evaluation of a Transaction. The term “Confidential Information” shall be deemed to include those portions of any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, memoranda or other documents (regardless of the form thereof) prepared by the receiving party or its Associates which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, any information furnished to the receiving party or its Associates pursuant hereto.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Confidential Information" with "Protected Information" in the section regarding use restrictions. This creates a contradiction because the rest of the agreement, including the contradicted text defining "Confidential Information", relies on the specific definition and usage of that term. By introducing a new term, "Protected Information," without defining it, the changed text creates ambiguity and potential conflict with the established definition of "Confidential Information." It is unclear if "Protected Information" is meant to include all, some, or none of what is considered "Confidential Information", directly conflicting with the detailed definition provided in the 'Contradicted Text'.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates and agrees, at its sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Associates from disclosure or use of the Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates and agrees, at its sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Affiliates from disclosure or use of the Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Associates" to "Affiliates" in the context of restraining disclosure obligations creates a contradiction. Associates are defined as having confidentiality agreements, while Affiliates may not. This creates confusion about the scope of the receiving party's responsibility. This contradicts with the first few sentences of the agreement where both the term is defined
perturbation.contradicted_text: HPE is evaluating forms of a potential transaction with respect to the Company (a “ Transaction ”), in connection with which each may disclose certain information, including Confidential 1nformation (as defined below), to the other. As a condition to such information being furnished to each party, its affiliates, its subsidiaries and other individuals and entities controlled, directly or indirectly, by such party (“ Affiliates ”), and the respective directors, employees, consultants, accountants, attorneys and advisors of such party and its Affiliates with which such party has entered into confidentiality agreements with material terms that are substantially similar to those in this Agreement (collectively with such Affiliates, “ Associates ”), each party agrees such information shall be provided to Associates for the limited purpose of evaluating, negotiating or consummating the Transaction, to treat the other party’s Confidential Information in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and to take or abstain from taking certain other actions, as described in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the receiving party must restrain its "Associates" from disclosing information. The changed text alters "Associates" to "Affiliates." The contradicted text defines "Associates" as "Affiliates" *with* confidentiality agreements. Since Affiliates are not necessarily bound by confidentiality agreements, the changed text broadens the scope of restraint to entities not necessarily subject to confidentiality, creating a contradiction because the original obligation only applied to entities with confidentiality agreements (Associates).

perturbation.original_text: Neither party will disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to third parties except those of its Associates who are required to have the information in order to evaluate and discuss a Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party will disclose any Sensitive Data of the other party to third parties except those of its Associates who are required to have the information in order to evaluate and discuss a Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Sensitive Data" only in this clause, without defining "Sensitive Data," leaves the reader unsure about what information is now restricted from disclosure. It raises the question of whether "Sensitive Data" is broader or narrower than "Confidential Information", contradicting the comprehensive definition of "Confidential Information" provided earlier. The definition of Sensitive data, not being present, leads to this in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information; Use and Disclosure . “ Confidential Information ” means any information, technical data or know-how, including, but not limited to, that which relates to research, product or service plans, business practices, agreement terms, products, services, employees, suppliers, customers, technology or other strategic partners, stockholders, markets, software, know-how, developments, inventions, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, notes, analyses or studies and all tangible and intangible embodiments thereof of any kind whatsoever, whether conveyed in writing or orally by the disclosing party or its Associates to the receiving party or its Associates in connection with the evaluation of a Transaction. The term “Confidential Information” shall be deemed to include those portions of any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, memoranda or other documents (regardless of the form thereof) prepared by the receiving party or its Associates which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, any information furnished to the receiving party or its Associates pursuant hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' which is specifically defined in the 'Contradicted Text' section. The changed text replaces this with 'Sensitive Data,' which is not defined anywhere in the document. This creates an in-text contradiction because the reader now doesn't know what 'Sensitive Data' encompasses, especially in relation to the defined 'Confidential Information.' This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition, which states that using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Each disclosing party acknowledges that the receiving party or its Associates may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the disclosing party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each disclosing party acknowledges that the receiving party or its Associates may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the disclosing entity’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Referring to the disclosing party as "disclosing entity" in this section (whereas elsewhere it is "disclosing party") introduces inconsistency. Since the term "disclosing entity" was only introduced in location 1 by replacing "disclosing party", the text contradicts in cases where the term is being reffered, thus creating ambiguity about which entity's Confidential Information is being referred to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A party disclosing Confidential Information hereunder is referred to herein as “ the disclosing party ” and a party receiving the Confidential Information of a disclosing party hereunder is referred to herein as “ the receiving party .”
perturbation.justification: The original text uses "disclosing party", which is defined in the contradicted text as the party disclosing Confidential Information. The changed text replaces "disclosing party" with "disclosing entity". This introduces a new term, "disclosing entity," that is not defined in the contradicted text. This creates ambiguity and a contradiction because it's unclear whether "disclosing entity" and "disclosing party" are intended to be the same, leading to potential confusion regarding whose Confidential Information is being referenced. The Misaligned Terminology definition applies here since there is a new term being used in place of the established term.

perturbation.original_text: Following written notice by one party to another of the termination of discussions between the parties, upon written request of the disclosing party, the receiving party shall take reasonable steps to instruct all persons involved in the Transaction to destroy all Confidential Information furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Following written notice by one party to another of the termination of discussions between the parties, upon written request of the disclosing business, the receiving party shall take reasonable steps to instruct all persons involved in the Transaction to destroy all Confidential Information furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Using the term "disclosing business" in place of "disclosing party" in this section introduces a new undefined term. This creates an in-text contradiction by raising the question of whether the request for destruction of confidential information only applies if the 'disclosing business' is requesting it and what the difference is between a party and a business in this context. It contradicts the previously defined roles of the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A party disclosing Confidential Information hereunder is referred to herein as “ the disclosing party ” and a party receiving the Confidential Information of a disclosing party hereunder is referred to herein as “ the receiving party .”
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'disclosing party' with 'disclosing business,' introducing a new, undefined term that deviates from the contract's established definition of 'disclosing party.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which defines 'disclosing party' and 'receiving party.' The use of 'disclosing business' creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding which entity can request the destruction of confidential information, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.
